261613.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow does not opt for a costly and energy-consuming civil service or administrative merger. Independence remains a priority. Cooperation happens pragmatically, on content and project level, without loss of local control.

261614.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow sees no added value in this form of participation. Residents, volunteers, organizations, and companies already participate in various ways in projects and policy making.

261615.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow is fine with residents solving problems in their living environment themselves with their own budget. Of course, this is done in coordination with neighborhood coordinators and managers of our public spaces.

261616.explanation: BN&M supports having a walk-in moment for residents to apply for or collect documents such as a driver's license or ID card without an appointment. This service fits well in the busy lives of residents.

261617.explanation: Residents know how to find the municipality well for all kinds of big and small problems, think of the BuitenBeter app, enforcement officers, and neighborhood coordinators. BN&M is in contact with many residents, associations, volunteers, and companies.

261618.explanation: The building code applies. As far as we are concerned, the municipality should not impose special additional requirements. If there are obstructive stricter requirements, we will investigate whether we can abolish them.

261619.explanation: This is legally not possible, thus not feasible and also unfair. Not every starting entrepreneur owns a property for which property tax is paid. Think of rental properties and home-working locations.

261620.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow finds it a sympathetic idea. At the same time, it is a task for the national government, like the previous subsidy for solar energy. By the way, the home battery still needs further development.

261621.explanation: Beuningen has a lot to offer with rivers, floodplains, recreational areas, and events. Beuningen Now & Tomorrow wants to make this more visible together with entrepreneurs and associations and support initiatives that make the municipality more attractive to tourists.

261622.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow does not want to expand the business park at this location in the important floodplain area for us. We want to keep this area open for nature, recreation, tourism, and agricultural activities.

261624.explanation: For the agricultural sector, we offer room for development with attention to nature, sustainability, and circular agriculture.

261625.explanation: The housing shortage is large. Because of this, we sometimes have to use agricultural land for building homes. Infill or connection to existing homes is our preference. We do not want large-scale housing development in the floodplain and riverbank areas.

261626.explanation: We are not in favor of destroying capital. It has already been decided to build new at Lèghe Polder, including a library and homes at the current location. This also applies to the renovation of the town hall. The Tinnegieter is currently being made more sustainable. This location is essential for school gymnastics.

261627.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow only wants trend-based increases of property tax, sewer charges, and waste disposal charges. Taxes will only rise if it is truly necessary and can be demonstrably justified.

261628.explanation: This is a task for the national government.

261630.explanation: We do not use this money only for housing Ukrainians, but especially also for our own residents, particularly urgent seekers and starters.

261631.explanation: The municipality is bound by national rules and regional agreements. In recent years, Beuningen has more than fulfilled its assignment. BN&M is making a housing plan in which all groups have a place, aiming for more homes for our own residents.

261632.explanation: BN&M believes we are already doing a lot. Beuningen commits itself more than sufficiently, carefully, humanely, and with respect. Integration only succeeds with language education, work, and opportunities to participate. This way we help people on their way without putting too much pressure on facilities.

261634.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow sees opportunities for a district heating network in Weurt and Beuningen near ARN, especially in neighborhoods with many (corporate) homes. We await the ongoing research and look at future energy sources. Residents are carefully involved in further steps.

261635.explanation: BN&M applies the 3-30-300 method: every home sees 3 trees, every neighborhood has 30% greenery, and everyone lives within 300 meters of a green space. This way we make neighborhoods climate resilient and strengthen biodiversity.

261636.explanation: We would like this, but the statement goes too far at the moment. We would then have to close many businesses, which is not possible. Of course, BN&M encourages companies to work as sustainably as possible, for example in permit requirements or when selling land.

261637.explanation: Where logical and appropriate, Beuningen Now & Tomorrow wants to replace grass strips with biodiverse green areas. This strengthens nature, enhances quality of life, and makes neighborhoods more climate resilient.

261638.explanation: BN&M believes this is justified, but visitors currently pay tourist tax only indirectly (via accommodations). Sometimes it can be arranged through agreements with landowners like Leisurelands or parties such as Mojo. We cannot currently charge extra costs separately to visitors.

261639.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow cherishes and supports all local events; from carnival, fairs, and VrijPop to Beuningen on Ice, spring festivals, mill festivals, the evening four-day marches, the Nijmegen Four Days Marches, and also neighborhood initiatives that strengthen social cohesion.

261640.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow does not want new energy projects such as additional wind turbines or solar fields; our contribution has been made. The new energy vision will still leave opportunities open for neighborhood and entrepreneurial initiatives for energy saving and storage.

261641.explanation: BN&M is investigating the swimming policy in the municipality of Beuningen. Every child (whose parents have a low income) must be able to obtain at least swimming diplomas A and B. This may be possible through the Klavervierpas.

261642.explanation: This is unaffordable without support from the national government. Beuningen Now & Tomorrow does not want to make preschool education free for all toddlers but does want to maintain a safety net for those with minimal income so every child receives the support they need.

261643.explanation: BN&M finds it essential that residents possess digital skills. Digital skills courses are therefore already arranged with municipal funds through the library, the Perspectief foundation, and senior citizens' associations.

261644.explanation: BN&M promotes exercise, healthy nutrition, and mental resilience through sports clubs, senior activities, and prevention programs. Seniors who can afford it contribute themselves so that support remains affordable for everyone.

261645.explanation: BN&M considers reading important at every age. We support schools in enhancing their book offerings. With extra national funds, every primary school will have its own library.

261646.explanation: Unfortunately, rudeness is increasing. Beuningen Now & Tomorrow wants to deploy an additional enforcement officer against rudeness, pollution, dumping, illegal parking, and nuisance. Visible presence of enforcement officers in villages and neighborhoods also works preventively.

261647.explanation: You may want it, but it is not possible. Camera surveillance is legally only allowed if earlier measures such as extra lighting, increased supervision, neighborhood watch, and targeted enforcement prove insufficient. It is an expensive (image data interpretation) part of a broader approach, not the first step.

261648.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow considers controls on traffic behavior and speed by police or enforcement officers important. However, it must apply to all road users. We find fatbikes too limited as a category.

261650.explanation: We are creating a new environmental program for housing construction between Beuningen-East and Weurt. Only after that will we know what housing is possible. Besides housing needs, environmental factors such as industry (ARN, sewage treatment plant), spraying zones near fruit growers, and the main gas pipeline also partly determine the possibilities.

261651.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow does not want houses on the riverbank levees along the Waal. Together with the floodplains, this is the most beautiful and valuable area of our municipality. Only in case of functional change, such as converting greenhouse horticulture or old barns, may some housing be appropriate.

261653.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow thoroughly agrees with this statement. Unfortunately, the municipality is bound to regional agreements. For new social rental homes, we choose lottery allocation with 50% of homes assigned to local residents.

261655.explanation: BN&M is in favor of new construction in De Lèghe Polder combined with apartments. After research, we will know if it is feasible and appropriate.

261656.explanation: BN&M, due to the housing shortage, supports investments in temporary housing for young people, such as tiny houses and container homes. However, we continue to prefer permanent solutions and building. We use funds left over from the national compensation for housing Ukrainians.

261658.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow prefers to see the number of people living in poverty decrease. However, poverty is determined by many factors and is not entirely within our control. We want to support people so that they sustainably stay out of poverty.

261659.explanation: BN&M sees that this is already possible: debts are consolidated into one creditor via debt counseling. After a period of good behavior, the municipality can cooperate with debt forgiveness. This way, we support residents in making a fresh start.

261660.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow wants a subsidy scheme for homeowners with a low income and poorly insulated homes. This follows the successful experience with the Energy Fix Bus, which in recent years helped many low-income households with free insulation.

261661.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow would like to do this. Some of our clients can do (part-time) work very well. Another portion often is no longer able to do paid or unpaid work. Volunteering is then the highest achievable. Tailored solutions remain necessary with focus on paid work.

261663.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow wants to make the Julianapassage a pedestrian zone. Shops should remain well accessible by bike for less mobile people. We are investigating options such as separate bike lanes and better bike parking facilities.

261664.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow wants to make a number of bike paths safer together with residents. Improving lighting is important. Bike paths near the lock in Weurt and Pieckelaan should be addressed together with Nijmegen. But also around the Beuningen lake and other unsafe places, action is needed.

261665.explanation: BN&M supports it if it turns out to be necessary and there is a demand. We must consider the availability of electricity. For us, connecting new homes takes priority over additional charging stations.

261666.explanation: BN&M wants stricter enforcement of blue-zone parking rules. Parking pressure in the center is increasing due to permanent parking without a permit or disk and due to more visitors. We are investigating enforcement with scan vehicles and intensifying deployment of enforcement officers.

261668.explanation: BN&M wants to promote bicycle use positively, not make car use unattractive, because not everyone can do their shopping by bike. Therefore, we improve bike paths, lighting, parking, and charging points.

261670.explanation: According to BN&M, a bike tunnel to sportpark Ooigraaf can improve accessibility. But we do not yet know if this is the best solution for the crossing problem. Therefore, we first investigate this and simultaneously look at the speed on the Schoenaker.

261671.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow contributed to the 30 km/h speed limit in all residential areas. So that is already in place. For good traffic flow and public transport, we want to keep the access roads to neighborhoods at 50 km/h.

261672.explanation: According to BN&M, this already happens via the "Social Assistance for Self-Employed" scheme. Entrepreneurs can get debt counseling there while retaining their business. We support this and will keep it accessible and well publicized for those who need it.

261674.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow keeps investing in good places for young people in every village. Every village already has community centers, sports canteens, and in Winssen and Beuningen facilities run by Perspectief. Think also of the realization of the new pumptrack in Beuningen/Ewijk.

261675.explanation: Beuningen Now & Tomorrow wants to examine the results of Perspectief in youth work, elderly work, poverty alleviation, volunteer work, and culture. Afterwards, we will reconsider the organization of welfare work and appropriate subsidies.

261613.explanation: An investigation into merging with Wijchen and Druten is needed. Cost reduction and better facilities for residents contribute to this becoming a realistic option.

261614.explanation: Participation is important. However, it is the council's role to engage in dialogue with residents and act as people's representatives.

261615.explanation: Residents know how to reach the municipality. To maintain broad support when deciding how money is spent, it is important that the council co-decides on budget allocation.

261616.explanation: This is currently not the case and a thorn in the side of residents. If you have obtained your driver's license or urgently need a new passport, it is good service to cooperate as a municipality.

261617.explanation: This is actually already happening. Additionally, it is once again the council's task to know what is happening in society.

261618.explanation: Fewer rules, more decisiveness. All the rules contribute to making building unnecessarily difficult in Beuningen. With fewer rules, more will be built and there is less administrative hassle to get a plan off the ground.

261619.explanation: Beuningen benefits from a healthy and diverse resident climate. A starting entrepreneur who starts a business for the first time should be encouraged through, for example, a property tax exemption.

261620.explanation: It is up to the resident how to arrange this themselves and financially not feasible for the vast majority of residents. Currently, an interesting loan is available if residents want to purchase a home battery.

261622.explanation: In the interest of cooperation with Wijchen, it is commendable that expansion of the business park is being considered.

261624.explanation: It is not the municipality's role to interfere in the free market, and in the very green municipality that Beuningen already is, there is no need to designate additional nature areas.

261625.explanation: We have a housing crisis. Entrepreneurs who want to think along and work on building homes that truly make a difference in solving the problem should receive cooperation.

261626.explanation: Cost savings, combating loneliness because many residents together, subsidy opportunities, a showcase, fewer volunteers needed, and accessibility. All are reasons why this is a good plan.

261627.explanation: The property tax (ozb) in the municipality is high. Too high in the eyes of the VVD. It should not increase, and certainly not rise above inflation.

261628.explanation: Safety-minded people do not fit into society and contribute to unrest wherever they are. In terms of safety, enforcement, and possibilities, this group does not fit in Beuningen.

261630.explanation: The money that has become available belongs to society. It must be used for what is important. Housing and safety are two important matters from which residents as well as Ukrainians will equally benefit.

261631.explanation: Everyone wants a home. A young person is also not eligible for a home without waiting time. Equal opportunity for housing for everyone unless there is an emergency situation.

261632.explanation: A lot of money is already spent on this. With the current resources, it should be sufficient to integrate into society.

261634.explanation: You cannot force residents to switch. It is up to the residents themselves. However, you can make agreements about this in new construction projects.

261635.explanation: It would be nice if this is feasible. But just like the rules, a three-tree rule that is mandatory also contributes to additional problems.

261636.explanation: Sustainability is very important, but if this is not yet possible due to innovation and technology, it is shortsighted to demand this from a company establishing itself when you want to build a gas-heated swimming pool yourself.

261637.explanation: Grass contributes to outdoor play. You also stimulate biodiversity by taking care of parks and green spaces.

261639.explanation: Completely agree. Subsidy opportunities and cooperation in organizing small-scale events contribute to a stronger society.

261640.explanation: No wind turbines in Beuningen as long as the health risks are not clear.

261641.explanation: The municipality is not responsible for this. Additionally, there are options for residents with a smaller budget to get this reimbursed.

261642.explanation: No distinction between needing and being for everyone.

261643.explanation: This is already being done. However, nothing has yet been done specifically for young people, and the municipality can play a meaningful role here.

261644.explanation: A lot of money is already being invested in this topic and you cannot be against that. But a good plan that requires money could change this again.

261645.explanation: We have a library in Beuningen where all young people have a free membership. Additionally, all primary schools have a library.

261646.explanation: Given the change in society and the decreased feeling of safety in the municipality, it is important to have more public order officers (boa’s). VVD Beuningen has advocated this for years; as far as we're concerned, Beuningen should have at least 4 or 5 boa’s.

261647.explanation: Entrepreneurs increasingly face nuisance. For residents and entrepreneurs, it is important that incidents are well mapped to take follow-up action and prevent recurrence.

261648.explanation: Fatbike riders regularly cause dangerous situations for our residents. If they follow the rules, there is nothing wrong. But dangerous fatbikes must be banned from the roads.

261650.explanation: This has never been discussed, but given the huge need for housing, VVD Beuningen considers it a topic worth discussing.

261651.explanation: If the situation allows, housing construction is definitely possible there. We base this statement on the Heemstraweg, above which several homes have already been built.

261653.explanation: This is not feasible given regional agreements, but we would absolutely not be against it.

261655.explanation: This has already been unanimously established.

261656.explanation: There are several entrepreneurs who have not received permits for building multiple Tiny houses. The municipality should actually support and contribute to the possibilities around these projects.

261658.explanation: There are sufficient arrangements for people with a smaller budget.

261659.explanation: This remains tailor-made. Reasons why residents are in debt are diverse, so universal policy is not possible.

261660.explanation: All residents deserve the same opportunities to obtain free insulation materials. Employed people can be just as financially limited to insulate as those with the lowest incomes.

261661.explanation: This statement is very black and white, but the municipality may expect as a counterpart for welfare benefits that residents contribute to society. If this is through sufficient volunteer work and is supervised, that is sufficient.

261663.explanation: Definitely during the opening hours of shops. When the shops are closed, this is unnecessary as long as there is no real hospitality area yet.

261664.explanation: We can no longer agree with a statement. All our students and other cyclists currently face an unsafe crossing and poor lighting, which should be addressed immediately.

261665.explanation: There are enough of these now and in the center, they are regularly occupied while other places are taken. Outside the center, you have to respond to the needs and demands of residents.

261666.explanation: Priority lies with safety. Enforcement is necessary but does not need to be intensified.

261668.explanation: There are sufficient bicycle parking spaces in the center.

261670.explanation: The costs involved in constructing a tunnel can be better spent on other methods that promote safety at all relevant bicycle crossings.

261671.explanation: Extending the 30 km/h zone to roads where accidents frequently occur is an option. Otherwise, this is not relevant to guarantee safety.

261672.explanation: If the company's activities contribute to faster recovery from debt counseling, it is necessary for the municipality to cooperate.

261674.explanation: Young people in Beuningen have almost no opportunity to meet each other. The municipality must play a facilitating role here by listening to and looking at the needs of the youth.

261675.explanation: With all the changes in society, it is important that Stichting Perspectief can continue to perform its tasks. We should encourage and subsidize a welfare organization that cares deeply about our municipality like Beuningen.

261613.explanation: There is currently no reason to actively work on a merger. However, we can seek more collaboration with our neighboring municipalities on certain topics where there is a win-win situation.

261614.explanation: A general citizens' assembly can help to engage residents in a low-threshold way and thus improve participation. By selecting a group of people objectively, a better conversation can take place, also on sensitive themes. Representation per district can also work well as a supplement.

261615.explanation: Strengthening the local districts is an important point. For this, the municipal council does not always need to engage in-depth. However, we must carefully examine how this money is then spent and who decides on it.

261616.explanation: There is no indication that current opening hours cause many problems. Keeping desks open also costs time and manpower, and we must manage that carefully. Being unnecessarily open is therefore a waste. Additionally, something can always be arranged if someone really needs it.

261617.explanation: Although this sounds very democratic, it also raises false expectations. The municipality must work on consultation and participation on all kinds of subjects, but there are many more democratic methods for this, such as walk-in consultation hours, a citizens' assembly, or council meetings on location.

261618.explanation: The municipality of Beuningen does not impose any strange or extra special requirements that are obstructive. There are various national rules we must also comply with, such as energy-efficient building standards, at least 30% social housing, etc.

261619.explanation: A sympathetic idea, but it is very questionable how much actual effect it has. Moreover, it is very difficult to implement and prone to fraud. There are better ways to help starting entrepreneurs.

261620.explanation: A home battery can help somewhat relieve grid congestion. But there are already national schemes for VAT refunds, among other things. At the moment, home batteries are not very profitable or effective. This (expensive) scheme would primarily benefit higher incomes.

261621.explanation: Although we welcome tourists, it is not necessary to invest extra money in this.

261622.explanation: According to GroenLinks-PvdA, the rural area should mainly remain green, with (organic) agricultural land or nature. There is currently no need for more industrial sites, at most a specific facility, such as possibly an energy station to solve issues on the electricity grid.

261624.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is always in favor of more nature and green areas. Vacant agricultural land is the ideal place for this. That certainly does not mean that, for example, compulsory buyouts are on the agenda in Beuningen.

261625.explanation: There must be construction in our municipality. But we have already designated several areas for this, and we should first build in those (such as Winssen and Ewijk). There will also be a plan for Beuningen East and Weurt. However, this does not necessarily mean that much agricultural land needs to be given up.

261626.explanation: Combining functions can be a good idea, but it must fit. Because many decisions have already been made, this is probably no longer realistic in this case. There are already advanced plans for both the town hall and the Leghe Polder. We should not disrupt that process any further.

261627.explanation: It is good to follow national price increases as much as possible, but this is not a strict rule. This may vary by year.

261628.explanation: Everyone who needs shelter and help always deserves it. But this issue is currently not relevant in Beuningen, so we do not need to arrange extra facilities for it. This is much more a task for a central municipality such as Nijmegen.

261630.explanation: Shelter is not a profit model, so this calculation is too simplistic. First and foremost, it concerns what kind of shelter is needed for whom and how the money is spent. Which budget is for what is secondary as long as we provide the right shelter.

261631.explanation: This is regulated by law. Moreover, in Beuningen, the numbers involved are very small and hardly affect the larger national problem (housing shortage, too few social homes).

261632.explanation: The municipality is already doing what it must do. Everyone who needs it, newcomer or longer-standing resident, must receive the help they need. Integration into our Beuningen society is important in this and therefore receives attention. The financial resources come from the national government.

261634.explanation: A district heating network is a good idea but unfortunately will not be realistic everywhere. It is more difficult to apply in existing construction than in new builds, and sufficient support is needed within a neighborhood.

261635.explanation: Except for a few exceptions, it is always a good idea to have more greenery and trees in residential areas. This helps against heat stress, is better for biodiversity, and residents become happier and healthier.

261636.explanation: All companies must comply with the rules applicable in the Netherlands. Additionally, it is good to encourage existing polluting companies to become cleaner and more sustainable. And we may certainly impose sustainable requirements on new companies wishing to establish themselves in our municipality.

261637.explanation: Although grass is certainly fine, it can be improved. Flower-rich grass mixtures increase biodiversity without requiring much investment. In addition, small flower beds and plants are a nice complement to this.

261638.explanation: Events already generate a lot of money through tourist taxes and agreements with the municipality. That money can also be used for preserving nature.

261639.explanation: Rules should not be an obstacle for residents. Good initiatives must therefore always be facilitated.

261640.explanation: This is not an issue in Beuningen. Over the coming years, we will (hopefully) first place five wind turbines, which will more than fulfill our task for the time being.

261641.explanation: It is a good thing that all children, regardless of origin or parents' income, learn to swim. Thanks in part to GroenLinks-PvdA, children from low-income families in Beuningen can already receive free swimming lessons through the Klavervierpas.

261642.explanation: Education is a right. It is good for a child's development to start at the right time, which must be possible regardless of origin or parents' income. This also fits within the current policy of the college of which GroenLinks-PvdA is part.

261643.explanation: For those who need it, the municipality can support becoming and remaining digitally skilled. This can be done via local libraries, walk-in consultation hours at the municipality, or possibly through (already existing) courses offered by a social organization in the municipality, for example in neighborhood centers or multifunctional centers (MFAs).

261644.explanation: A lot is already done for the elderly and sports facilities. But there is always room for more.

261645.explanation: Learning to read is extremely important for all children. A local library (at school or in a community center) can greatly help with this. We have already invested in this in the municipality in recent years and will continue to do so.

261646.explanation: More control may seem better by default, but we must carefully consider the need. If there is a need, funds can be allocated. However, we must be cautious with excessive control.

261647.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA is against standard camera surveillance. Already, limited camera surveillance can be used on the mayor's order if the situation requires it. That is sufficient for us now, and permanent camera surveillance is unnecessary.

261648.explanation: The municipality is already structurally consulting with the safety triangle, and this topic also comes up there. The same applies to scooters, dangerous behavior by cars, and other topics.

261650.explanation: After Winssen and Ewijk have been expanded with new homes, we will continue. Then we will look at Beuningen East and Weurt to see what space there is to build additional homes. But these will not be large-scale projects with hundreds of homes in this green strip.

261651.explanation: For GroenLinks-PvdA, the riverbank area is an important protected and characteristic (nature) area. In principle, no further construction should take place there. Functional changes are discussable in some cases.

261653.explanation: As far as the law permits, you can (to a limited extent) give priority to residents from your own municipality. Limited preferential treatment, and certainly urgency, is possible, but we must not lock up the housing market and should remain part of and collaborate within the region to jointly solve the problems.

261655.explanation: If that fits into the yet-to-be-developed plans, then it is a good idea.

261656.explanation: The municipality must provide housing for all target groups, and no solution can be excluded in this. Municipalities themselves do not invest money in temporary housing. However, they do facilitate and encourage others (housing corporations) to do so.

261658.explanation: Help must always remain accessible for people who need it. GroenLinks-PvdA does not want to cut back on provisions at the expense of the vulnerable in our municipality. Hopefully, the number of people needing help will decrease, not by making the provisions harder to access but by lifting these residents out of poverty.

261659.explanation: The municipality cannot forgive debts, but it can assist and support debt restructuring and similar processes. Results from a pilot elsewhere have so far (unfortunately) not been encouraging.

261660.explanation: The energy transition must not work only for higher incomes. Where possible, the municipality encourages (also financially) schemes that allow everyone to participate in making their homes more sustainable. The municipality already does this.

261661.explanation: Social assistance is not intended as payment for a counter-performance but as an income provision for those who need it. Work experience helps to return to paid employment, but volunteer work can be just as useful; for the individual and for us as a society.

261663.explanation: More areas can certainly be created where cyclists, scooters, and/or fatbikes are banned. But we do not want to be patronizing and certainly want to keep the center attractive and accessible by bike. In the passage, however, cyclists are prohibited.

261664.explanation: We are definitely in favor of this, but it must be done in cooperation with the municipality of Nijmegen, as this part of the cycling route belongs to them. Additionally, GroenLinks/PvdA wants to invest in better and safer cycling connections with Wijchen and Bergharen.

261665.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports facilitating more electric cars, which includes charging stations. But of course, we must not overdo it by installing too many where there is no demand.

261666.explanation: Parking remains a contentious issue. Although at certain times there seems to be a shortage of spaces, we must also maintain a customer-friendly center. Enforcement is good and can be better than it currently is, but we should not overdo it either.

261668.explanation: The center can definitely be made more attractive for cyclists, for example by adding more bike parking, charging spots for electric bikes, and better access routes and bike paths around the center. But this does not necessarily have to come at the expense of car accessibility.

261670.explanation: The crossing must and can be made safer. A cycling tunnel sounds nice but is very expensive, and we believe there are better and equally safe solutions. Moreover, a cycling tunnel could potentially become an unsafe hangout spot.

261671.explanation: In residential neighborhoods, except for through roads and public transport routes, the maximum speed can be 30 km/h in almost all places. This is already policy in our municipality, partly thanks to GroenLinks-PvdA.

261672.explanation: Customization must be possible. Debt counseling can therefore consider all boundary conditions. But it does not have to be standard policy.

261674.explanation: Young people have the right to grow up in a safe environment with their own facilities and peers. Together with these youths, the municipality must work on places where they can (safely) spend time without causing nuisance to others.

261675.explanation: Stichting Perspectief does an incredible amount of good on behalf of and within our municipality. We must not cut funding for this. Therefore, an organization like Perspectief needs financial security, which the municipality can provide.

261613.explanation: The CDA focuses on municipal cooperation on regional issues.

261614.explanation: The CDA believes this should not be done by lottery. The CDA wants to appoint neighborhood contact persons who know what is happening in the village, bringing local government closer to residents, and that each neighborhood or village has its own budget.

261615.explanation: The CDA believes that minor defects in public spaces can be repaired by residents themselves using a budget. This works faster and easier and creates a bond between residents and their own living environment.

261616.explanation: The CDA believes that the town hall should be a walk-in center where you can come both with or without an appointment.

261617.explanation: CDA Beuningen stands for livability in the cores and therefore wants to appoint village contact persons who know what is happening in the village, bringing local government closer to residents, and that every village has a village council with its own budget.

261618.explanation: CDA Beuningen wants accelerated housing construction. National regulations suffice.

261619.explanation: Starting entrepreneurs have little financial space at first. By temporarily exempting them from property tax (ozb), they get more chance to build their business, which leads to more employment and a stronger local economy.

261620.explanation: There will be a national subsidy program for home batteries. The CDA supports collective purchase programs.

261621.explanation: More promotion does not solve problems. Invest in livability, events, and facilities. Then visitors will come naturally.

261622.explanation: For CDA Beuningen, this area is an indispensable green lung in our municipality. This unique area is of great value for both nature and agriculture, and we must handle it carefully with limited space for housing development.

261624.explanation: Agricultural land is valuable for food production, landscape, and nature. We must handle it carefully and sparingly. This must also be done in consultation with the farmer.

261625.explanation: We give housing priority when agricultural land becomes available, for example when a business is closed.

261626.explanation: The municipal council has decided on rebuilding the town hall and new construction of the village hall on the current site. If voters wish, the CDA is open to investigating a single location.

261627.explanation: Keeping the property tax (ozb) as low as possible since it directly affects the purchasing power of families and households. Therefore, we support a cap on the ozb linked to inflation. The ozb will not increase by more than inflation in the coming period.

261628.explanation: These people should be sent back to their country of origin according to national legislation.

261630.explanation: Money reserved for the reception of Ukrainians remains initially intended for this purpose. Any leftover funds will be used for starter housing for residents of our municipality.

261631.explanation: Beuningen meets the national task allocation. Therefore, the CDA believes we must first focus on housing for our own residents.

261632.explanation: There are already provisions for this. The municipality is doing enough, partly thanks to the efforts of Stichting Perspectief and Stichting VluchtelingenWerk.

261634.explanation: A district heating network is not the best solution everywhere. Residents must have freedom of choice, and solutions must be technically feasible, affordable, and reliable per village and neighborhood.

261635.explanation: Greenery is important, but fixed numbers per street are unrealistic. It's about the right tree in the right place, considering safety, space, and maintenance.

261636.explanation: A strict exclusion backfires. Sustainability is better achieved through clear agreements via permits and customization so that companies can grow and become more sustainable.

261637.explanation: Biodiversity is important, but completely replacing grass is not appropriate everywhere. Choose targeted greening in suitable places with attention to use, maintenance, and safety.

261638.explanation: The organizer is responsible for a careful event. Make clear agreements with organizers about handling nature and the environment. Additional visitor charges are undesirable and unnecessarily raise barriers to participation.

261639.explanation: Small-scale events strengthen community life and solidarity. Fewer rules and faster procedures make organizing easier without compromising safety and livability. The cancellation of Beuningen On Ice due to regulations must not happen again.

261640.explanation: New large-scale wind turbines and solar parks do not fit with Beuningen. Solar panels should be installed on roofs as much as possible to preserve the landscape and space for farmers. If wind energy is used, it should be small-scale and supported locally, for example via small wind turbines on farms.

261641.explanation: Swimming skills are essential for the safety of children. The municipality must facilitate this in a way that fits well within education and remains feasible for schools.

261642.explanation: Preschool education gives children a good start. Ensure that the basics are in order and provide support where necessary, so that developmental delays are reduced and equal opportunities are strengthened.

261643.explanation: Through Stichting Perspectief and the library, the municipality already arranges digital skills.

261644.explanation: Sports and physical activity for the elderly work preventively and promote social interaction. Investing in this helps support healthy aging and prevent loneliness.

261645.explanation: Reading is essential for language development. The library is now free to access. With tailored approaches (specific learning needs of individual children) and targeted investments, the municipality can help primary schools build a suitable digital school library.

261646.explanation: More enforcement officers can contribute to safety and livability, provided they are deployed in a focused and visible manner, for example in enforcing regulations against drug waste and dumping in rural areas.

261647.explanation: Camera surveillance is a severe measure and affects residents’ privacy. First focus on visible presence, enforcement, and prevention before opting for permanent cameras.

261648.explanation: Fatbikes and similar vehicles increasingly cause unsafe situations. Expanding targeted controls on behavior and speed to this group improves traffic safety, especially for children and the elderly. CDA Beuningen says: stores open, cyclists walk.

261650.explanation: Currently, the emphasis of housing plans is mainly in Ewijk and Winssen. A new neighborhood between Beuningen-East and Weurt provides better distribution of housing within the municipality and meets the need to also build in and around Beuningen and Weurt.

261651.explanation: The river banks along the Waal are valuable for landscape and cultural history. Housing does not fit there; the preference is for other and better-suited locations within the municipality.

261655.explanation: Combining the new construction of De Lèghe Polder with apartments creates a financially feasible project and makes living in the center possible, close to facilities and meeting places.

261656.explanation: Temporary housing forms such as tiny houses or container homes quickly offer young people prospects for their own place. This relieves housing pressure and creates space for flow-through.

261658.explanation: Arrangements for help with poverty must remain focused on those who need it. Support for this must not be cut. Facilities such as the food bank remain indispensable for residents who need temporary or long-term assistance.

261659.explanation: The municipality must focus on support such as budget management, help with installment payments, and utilizing schemes and subsidies so that people come out of problems sustainably.

261660.explanation: Households with the lowest incomes often live in social rental housing. The responsibility for insulation lies with housing corporations. The municipality’s role is to make clear agreements on this, manage implementation, and ensure these improvements are actually realized.

261661.explanation: People receiving social assistance can be expected to provide a return in the form of paid work or volunteer work. Those who already do volunteer work contribute socially and do not need to perform additional compulsory unpaid work.

261663.explanation: CDA Beuningen in the shopping center: stores open, cyclists walk. An important precondition is the expansion of bicycle racks.

261664.explanation: The CDA believes that the bike paths near the Weurt lock and Pieckelaan must be safer. These routes are intensively used by school children and heavy traffic (from sand mining) and are often dark and unclear due to poor lighting.

261665.explanation: More charging points make electric driving more accessible and support the transition to more sustainable transport, provided this is done carefully and does not increase parking pressure in residential areas.

261666.explanation: Rules only make sense if they are enforced. Stricter enforcement is needed so that the blue zone serves its intended purpose: flow. Long-term parking must not come at the expense of residents. Therefore, where necessary, additional parking spaces for residents must be arranged.

261668.explanation: The center of Beuningen is currently well balanced between cars and bicycles. Good accessibility for visitors from outside Beuningen remains important for shops and employment. Promoting cycling is fine, but not at the expense of that balance.

261670.explanation: The CDA considers a bicycle tunnel necessary to make sport park Ooigraaf safe and well accessible. Many children and adults cycle here daily to football, hockey, and korfball, and during major events such as Beuningen On Ice and soon the new swimming pool, the crowds increase.

261671.explanation: 30 km/h is appropriate in residential areas and streets where people live and play. Access roads in residential areas, such as Houtduiflaan, Burgemeester Geradtslaan, and Wilhelminalaan, must remain 50 km/h to ensure flow and accessibility.

261672.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be able to apply for debt assistance while keeping their business, provided the business is viable. Debt assistance should focus on healthy continuation or a realistic future perspective, not artificially maintaining an unviable business.

261674.explanation: Young people need their own safe place to meet each other. This strengthens their connection to the village and prevents boredom. A meeting place in every core shows that Beuningen, Weurt, Winssen, and Ewijk all count.

261675.explanation: Yes, investing in welfare is good — but must be sensible and verifiable. Welfare organization Perspectief plays an important role. A higher fixed subsidy is possible, provided clear goals are agreed upon, the municipality retains control, and efforts and effects are regularly evaluated.

261613.explanation: In the long term, a merger with neighboring municipalities is a logical step for us. Not to make Beuningen an anonymous city, but to ensure a strong organization that delivers quality and remains locally rooted.

261614.explanation: This has no added value for Beuningen.

261615.explanation: We are in favor of this, as with the village deal in Winssen.

261616.explanation: An agreement is necessary to work effectively.

261618.explanation: Rules should not hinder fast construction.

261619.explanation: Good to strengthen the starter climate.

261620.explanation: Clean energy and investments in the transition must be encouraged.

261621.explanation: The municipality has sufficient appeal and facilitates an adequate budget.

261622.explanation: This fits our vision of a good climate for entrepreneurs.

261624.explanation: The municipality must ensure a good balance between nature, agriculture, and industry.

261625.explanation: Building is necessary to provide every resident with a home.

261626.explanation: New construction and renovation are necessary for all buildings, but at our own pace and planning.

261627.explanation: It is not necessary to increase property tax (OZB) further, and life is already expensive enough for many people.

261628.explanation: This is not an issue in Beuningen.

261630.explanation: The municipality has the task of housing all groups. The money should be used for this purpose.

261631.explanation: The municipality has a legal obligation and fulfills this in cooperation with the housing corporations.

261634.explanation: This is not realistic. The municipality can advise residents to take appropriate measures.

261635.explanation: This improves the quality of life in the villages.

261636.explanation: This is not feasible for all business sectors.

261637.explanation: There must be a good balance in greenery. In addition to space for biodiversity, green areas for playing are also important.

261638.explanation: Polluters must pay. The question is whether this is already regulated.

261639.explanation: Good for the livability in villages.

261640.explanation: The current plans for wind and solar energy are sufficient.

261641.explanation: Obtaining a swimming diploma is the responsibility of parents, not the schools. When parents cannot afford this, there is the child package.

261642.explanation: We invest in all children based on inclusion.

261643.explanation: This is already provided by the library.

261644.explanation: The municipality can have a facilitating role when it comes to offering recreational sports.

261645.explanation: Reading is a basic skill that should be supported this way at every school.

261646.explanation: The current community officers are sufficient.

261647.explanation: There is no direct reason for such an extensive measure.

261650.explanation: This is included in the housing vision and offers opportunities for the development of the municipality.

261651.explanation: This is a vulnerable area where building can only take place with customized solutions.

261653.explanation: We want to help young home seekers within established laws and regulations.

261655.explanation: This is a beautiful combination of living and culture in the center of Beuningen.

261656.explanation: We want to help young people get started on the housing market within the boundaries of the municipality of Beuningen.

261658.explanation: The assistance provided is effective and must therefore be continued.

261659.explanation: Buying up properties is an effective way to help people, provided it is done under the right conditions.

261660.explanation: There are sufficient subsidies and options to borrow at a low rate for this purpose.

261661.explanation: This has no proven effect on encouraging paid employment.

261663.explanation: This increases safety in the center.

261664.explanation: There are good segregated bike paths on the route towards Weurt and Nijmegen.

261665.explanation: The municipality must ensure a good balance between charging points and regular parking spaces.

261666.explanation: Enforcement of rules is important to prevent nuisance.

261668.explanation: Good parking facilities on the edge of the center are important to ensure a center that is pleasant to stay in. We continue to monitor this, but no additional measures are needed at this time.

261670.explanation: This increases the safety of our youth who play sports at the Ooijgraaf.

261671.explanation: This is important for traffic safety and the environment.

261672.explanation: Under the right conditions, this is an important means to support entrepreneurs.

261674.explanation: There are sufficient meeting places available. Of course, every initiative can be considered.

261675.explanation: A higher annual subsidy does not contribute to the effectiveness of welfare work.

261613.explanation: If this is the municipality's wish, residents must have a say in it, for example through a referendum.

261614.explanation: In Beuningen, residents must be involved at an early stage in major decisions.

261615.explanation: The municipality has a facilitating role in this.

261616.explanation: To offer residents more flexibility, the town hall should be open more often outside office hours. Additionally, there should be times when residents can come by without an appointment.

261617.explanation: This is the role of the political parties themselves.

261618.explanation: The requirements must be simplified in order to accelerate the construction of new homes. This must not come at the expense of at least 30% social housing.

261619.explanation: Depending on the type and purpose of the company.

261620.explanation: To relieve the energy grid as much as possible, we encourage the purchase of a home battery.

261624.explanation: This must not come at the expense of housing construction.

261625.explanation: Taking into account at least 30% social rental housing.

261626.explanation: It is not realistic to get all of this under one roof.

261627.explanation: The broadest shoulders carry the heaviest burdens.

261628.explanation: The Dispersion Act must be applied.

261631.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit are entitled to urgency status equal to urgent cases from the community.

261632.explanation: The better newcomers integrate into society, the less this leads to nuisance.

261634.explanation: Only if this is financially and operationally feasible.

261637.explanation: There must be as many places as possible to encourage playing outside.

261638.explanation: Events in the countryside impact nature, and a financial contribution may be required in return.

261639.explanation: Small events promote social contacts among people and create greater cohesion.

261640.explanation: Wind turbines are only negotiable if it demonstrably serves the public interest, not because a lobby wants it. For solar panels, we first use the existing space: roofs of businesses, public buildings, corporations, and homes.

261641.explanation: School swimming must be structurally organized and linked to the new swimming pool, so that every child in Beuningen learns to swim and water is not a danger.

261642.explanation: Every child deserves appropriate education and care.

261643.explanation: The municipality must promote the use of digital services by offering training in this area.

261644.explanation: The more vital the elderly remain, the less pressure there is on healthcare.

261645.explanation: This is a task for the overarching organization. The municipality provides the public library for primary schools.

261647.explanation: By expanding enforcement, this is not necessary.

261648.explanation: By making the center a pedestrian zone, you already keep fatbikes out of the center.

261653.explanation: Everyone has equal opportunities for social rental housing, except for people with an urgency declaration.

261656.explanation: There must be more social rental housing so that these solutions are not needed.

261658.explanation: Help with poverty must not be limited by a maximum number of people but should depend on income.

261659.explanation: The municipality must provide active debt assistance to those who need it.

261660.explanation: The municipality must provide small-scale insulation aids. For major insulation maintenance of social rental homes, the landlord is responsible.

261661.explanation: People on social assistance who do volunteer work already contribute something to society.

261663.explanation: For pedestrian safety, it is necessary to ban bicycles and fatbikes from the center.

261664.explanation: Extra lighting is needed in these places.

261665.explanation: This is only necessary if there is "charging pressure."

261666.explanation: By expanding enforcement, there is more room to supervise this.

261668.explanation: More bicycle parking facilities must be created in combination with establishing a pedestrian zone in the core of the center.

261670.explanation: This is not necessary due to the costs involved.

261672.explanation: Debts arising from a business are not personal debts for which debt assistance is intended.

261613.explanation: We are against a merger because it increases the distance between residents and government. Our municipality knows the neighborhoods and villages best, whereas mergers often lead to bureaucracy, higher costs, and loss of local identity. Democracy works strongest close to people where they are heard.

261614.explanation: We are against a citizen's assembly. Our concern is that it raises expectations that may not always be met. Within our municipality, besides the municipal council, there is space for a youth council and a senior council. VIER Beuningen maintains good contacts with the residents.

261615.explanation: We are against a village budget within our municipality. VIER Beuningen's vision is that small problems should be solved through a central desk without individual budgets per district. And it certainly should not lead to more bureaucracy and costs.

261616.explanation: The town hall should be open more often and without an appointment so that residents can easily come with their questions and matters. Not everyone can or wants to arrange everything digitally, and personal contact remains important for good service. This should also apply to the construction site.

261617.explanation: We are against working with a single contact person. VIER Beuningen is accessible to all residents of the municipality. In addition, within our municipality we have the social team and a community police officer. VIER Beuningen sees added value in Neighborhood Connectors.

261618.explanation: We support abolishing special stricter requirements that Beuningen imposes on the construction of new homes. By keeping rules simple and clear, projects can start faster and homes become available sooner for starters, families, and seniors.

261619.explanation: We believe that starting businesses should not have to pay property tax (ozb) for their first two years. This way, we give entrepreneurs space to grow and build their business. This makes our municipality more attractive for settlement. A strong local economy begins with this, among other things.

261620.explanation: We believe the municipality should not provide additional subsidies for home batteries. The municipality has already provided subsidies for energy-saving measures. Further subsidies should therefore be granted by the national government.

261621.explanation: We do not want to invest more money in promoting tourism. We are a residential municipality, and our resources should primarily be used for the livability, facilities, and safety of our residents. Our floodplains and riverbanks are already well known as recreational areas.

261622.explanation: We are against expanding this area. The agricultural meadow area should remain as open as possible in our opinion.

261624.explanation: We agree with this. If a farmer stops, we want to encourage that these lands are converted into nature reserves.

261626.explanation: We are not in favor of a combined building. Critically look at how services can connect with each other and seek cooperation and connection there.

261627.explanation: We agree that it should not increase more sharply. The municipality has its finances well in order and there is sufficient room to budget responsibly without extra pressure on residents. This keeps living and life in our municipality affordable for everyone.

261628.explanation: We are against temporary shelter for rejected asylum seekers in our municipality. There should be one location in the country (for example near Schiphol) where they can temporarily stay.

261630.explanation: We believe that the leftover funds from the previous shelter for Ukrainians should be spent on solving the housing shortage for all home seekers.

261631.explanation: We do not think that refugees should automatically have priority for social rental homes in our municipality. Allocation must be fair and careful, based on clear criteria and priorities so that all residents have an equal chance of suitable housing.

261632.explanation: We believe the municipality should not spend extra money on the integration of newcomers into society. The municipality already undertakes many actions to promote integration.

261634.explanation: VIER Beuningen believes that the alternative to natural gas does not necessarily have to be the heat network, as we look at the national trend.

261635.explanation: We agree that many trees should be planted in our municipality. Therefore, in 2024 it was decided that we aim for one tree per resident.

261636.explanation: We support the establishment of new companies in our municipality. It is important that they focus on sustainability.

261637.explanation: Going from grass to herbs is good for nature, the climate, residents, and maintenance costs. We agree with this. There should be room to deviate from this where needed.

261638.explanation: The rural area is vulnerable and valuable, and extra pressure from events must not come at the expense of nature, landscape, and livability. This way we ensure that recreation and protection go hand in hand.

261639.explanation: Fewer rules, faster permit issuance, and clear conditions ensure that initiatives from residents and associations get more space. This stimulates meeting, solidarity, and liveliness in our villages and neighborhoods.

261640.explanation: We believe no more wind turbines and solar parks should be built in our municipality. Several are already being installed, and with this, we contribute adequately to the energy transition and meet the obligations of the regional energy strategy (RES). Balance is important.

261641.explanation: We do not think the municipality is responsible for every child obtaining a swimming diploma. That responsibility primarily lies with parents (guardians). Parents with low incomes can use the Klavervierpas. Achieving this is a costly matter for the municipality.

261642.explanation: VIER Beuningen believes all toddlers who need preschool education should receive it. Besides 2 mornings of toddler care, the GGD can issue a VVE indication. Non-working parents will be reimbursed for this, and working parents can use the Childcare Act.

261643.explanation: We support offering a course to learn how to deal with digital services. This helps residents to independently and safely use online government services, care portals, and other digital applications. This increases self-reliance.

261644.explanation: We would like the municipality to allocate more funds for sport and exercise for the elderly. Investing in an active lifestyle contributes to health, independence, and well-being and leads to long-term savings in healthcare. This ensures elderly people remain vital and involved.

261645.explanation: VIER Beuningen believes not every primary school needs its own library. Cooperation, customization, and efficient use of existing facilities can be equally effective. Furthermore, it requires attention from schools that must manage and keep the library up to date.

261646.explanation: We would like to see more special enforcement officers (boa’s) working in our municipality. Extra supervision ensures better enforcement of rules, increases safety, and promotes livability in neighborhoods and villages. This makes residents feel safer and relieves the police.

261647.explanation: We believe there should be more camera surveillance in our village center, provided it complies with laws and regulations. Well-placed surveillance can reduce nuisance and crime and provides a safer feeling for residents and visitors without unnecessarily violating privacy.

261648.explanation: We support the municipality pushing the police more to monitor fatbike riders for their behavior and speed. This increases safety for all road users without nuisance or danger to others.

261650.explanation: VIER Beuningen believes a new residential area should be developed between Beuningen East and Weurt. This is a solution to the housing shortage in the municipality and strengthens the cohesion between Beuningen and Weurt.

261651.explanation: VIER Beuningen believes each situation should be considered to determine whether housing can be built in the Oeverwallen. These places should not be fully built up. This environment should remain as open and green as possible to preserve nature, landscape, and living environment.

261653.explanation: We find it very important that young home seekers from our own municipality get priority. This offers starters a fair chance to continue living in their own village. These young people have ties to the municipality and often contribute to community life.

261655.explanation: VIER Beuningen believes that the new construction of De Lèghe Polder can be combined with apartments. This aligns with the idea that buildings may be taller than four floors. Choices must be made to solve the housing shortage. This can also reduce costs.

261656.explanation: Our municipality should make it possible to allow temporary housing in tiny houses or container homes. This quickly provides extra housing, helps reduce the housing shortage, and offers residents and starters an affordable alternative while structural solutions are being worked on.

261658.explanation: We do not find it right to say that the number of people who can use poverty aid schemes should always remain the same. If it appears that more people need our help, we must create room for that. It is not about numbers but about a human approach.

261659.explanation: To some extent, the municipality must assist people who have fallen into financial trouble. Clear conditions and rules are necessary to ensure help is provided fairly and responsibly and that people needing support are actually helped.

261660.explanation: People with low incomes should receive support to make their homes more sustainable. This must be carefully considered; distributing standard insulation materials is not the right approach. It is about customization to offer the right solutions that truly contribute to energy saving.

261661.explanation: VIER Beuningen believes that people on social assistance who already do volunteer work should not be required to do unpaid work for the municipality. Through volunteering, people remain active, gain work experience, and maintain structure in daily life.

261663.explanation: This makes the center safer, more attractive, and better accessible for visitors and residents. Moreover, it stimulates local shops and restaurants and provides a pleasant meeting place in the heart of our municipality.

261664.explanation: Safety always comes first, and well-designed bike paths protect cyclists, encourage sustainable transportation, and make it pleasant for everyone to move around the municipality. Safety should never be up for debate.

261665.explanation: This number should increase over time, as the number of electric vehicles may rise. This way, we make sustainable transportation easier and promote the use of clean energy. It should be feasible considering grid congestion.

261666.explanation: VIER Beuningen believes that strict enforcement of the blue parking disc is unnecessary. Enforcement requires capacity and resources. By not deploying these on parking disc checks, Beuningen can prioritize more important tasks such as safety and nuisance prevention.

261668.explanation: We would like both cyclists and motorists to easily find their way to the center and be able to park there well. Clear routes and sufficient parking spaces ensure an accessible, clear, and attractive center for residents and visitors.

261670.explanation: In our opinion, a bike tunnel to our sports park is not always a safe solution. Other options should be considered so that cyclists and pedestrians can reach the sports park safely and reliably, without unnecessary risks or dangerous situations.

261671.explanation: We believe that the maximum speed limit in our municipality should be 30 km/h. Lower speeds increase traffic safety, make streets more pleasant for cyclists and pedestrians, and contribute to a livable and quiet environment for everyone.

261672.explanation: VIER Beuningen believes that entrepreneurs should be able to receive debt counseling while retaining their business. We are thinking of sole proprietors and self-employed persons. Temporary support gives the entrepreneur opportunities to overcome problems while keeping the business.

261674.explanation: We believe that each village should have its own place for our youth. This provides them a safe and accessible environment to meet, engage in activities, and develop themselves, contributing to involvement and community spirit in the village. We would like to engage in dialogue with the youth.

261675.explanation: VIER Beuningen finds it important to continue supporting Perspectief. It is an important organization for our municipality. Annual fixed subsidies usually come with performance agreements and evaluations, ensuring transparency and control.

statements.title: The maximum speed should be set to 30 km/h in all districts and neighborhoods.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.

titleParts.text: moet in alle wijken en buurten naar 30 km/u.

statements.title: The special strict requirements that the municipality of Beuningen imposes on the construction of new homes should be abolished.
statements.moreInfo: There is national legislation concerning housing construction. Municipalities are free to set additional local requirements. The municipality of Beuningen imposes extra requirements on housing regarding sustainability, density, greenery and types of homes.

statements.title: The municipality of Beuningen should merge with one of the neighboring municipalities.

titleParts.glossary: The merging of two or more municipalities.

statements.moreInfo: In this case, merging means that the municipality of Beuningen joins with another municipality to form one new, larger municipality with a single administration, budget and policy.

statements.title: The municipality should appoint a contact person in each village who reports on what is happening there.
All villages in the municipality of Beuningen should, as much as possible, switch to the district heating network as an alternative to natural gas.

titleParts.text: Alle dorpen in de gemeente Beuningen moeten zoveel mogelijk overstappen op het

titleParts.glossary: Network of underground pipes that brings hot water from a central source to homes and buildings, for both heating and tap water.

statements.moreInfo: A district heating network is an underground pipe system that transports hot water from a central source to homes and buildings. It provides heating and hot water and is an alternative to gas-fired boilers.

statements.title: The business park between the A73, Schoenaker and the Elsenpas may be expanded to the south of the A73.
statements.moreInfo: The De Schoenaker business park is located in the village of Beuningen.

statements.title: Households with the lowest incomes should receive free insulation materials from the municipality to save on energy.

titleParts.text: De huishoudens met de laagste inkomens moeten van de gemeente gratis

titleParts.glossary: Material in roofs, walls and floors that keeps heat in or out. In winter it keeps heat inside and in summer outside.

titleParts.text: krijgen om te besparen op energie.

statements.theme: Reception of Ukrainians (accommodation for Ukrainian refugees)
statements.title: The money the municipality had left over from accommodating Ukrainians should be used for permanent housing for refugees.
The town hall should be open more often so people can obtain a driver's license or ID without an appointment.
The municipality should financially help each primary school to assemble its own library.

titleParts.text: helpen om een eigen bibliotheek samen te stellen.

statements.title: Starting entrepreneurs should not have to pay property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) for the first two years.

titleParts.text: Startende ondernemers hoeven de eerste 2 jaar geen

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.moreInfo: Property tax is a local tax in the Netherlands that you pay to the municipality if you own a house, a commercial building or a piece of land.

statements.title: The municipality should ensure that primary school children can at least obtain swimming certificates A and B.
In public places in the municipality of Beuningen, grass should be replaced with flowers and other plants to improve biodiversity.

titleParts.text: Op openbare plekken in de gemeente Beuningen moet het gras worden vervangen door bloemen en andere planten om de

titleParts.glossary: There are many different species of plants, animals and fungi on Earth. Species depend on each other to keep nature healthy. More biodiversity means there are more species of plants and animals.

statements.title: There should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in the municipality of Beuningen. There are currently three.

titleParts.text: In de gemeente Beuningen moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

statements.title: The municipality should buy up or forgive the debts of low-income people who have severe financial problems.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet schulden opkopen of

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.

titleParts.text: van mensen met een laag inkomen die grote financiële problemen hebben.

statements.title: No homes may be built on the riverbanks of the Waal along the northern boundary of the municipality.
statements.moreInfo: The oeverwal (riverbank ridge) area is the landscape type found north of the Van Heemstraweg up to the dike. The area contains sandy soils.

statements.title: Only companies that operate sustainably should be allowed to establish themselves in our municipality from now on.

titleParts.glossary: Something you can (re)use for a long time.

titleParts.text: werken mogen zich voortaan in onze gemeente

titleParts.glossary: To build; to establish or set up (a building, organization or household).

statements.title: Camera surveillance should be installed in the center of Beuningen.

titleParts.text: In het centrum van Beuningen moet

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should more strictly enforce compliance with the blue parking disc rules.
When allocating a social rental home, young applicants from the municipality itself should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten jonge woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should make the center of Beuningen more attractive for cyclists at the expense of cars.
People receiving social assistance who do volunteer work should additionally perform unpaid work on behalf of the municipality.
The municipality should encourage agricultural land in the municipality to be used for housing.
The municipality should give the welfare organization Perspectief a higher fixed, annually recurring subsidy.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet welzijnsorganisatie Perspectief een hogere vaste, jaarlijks terugkerende

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

statements.moreInfo: Perspectief is the organization within the municipality of Beuningen that works for the welfare of all residents. It does this, among other things, by organizing activities for young and old.

statements.title: The municipality should prepare a plan for one new building for the town hall, the library, village hall De Lèghe Polder, and sports hall De Tinnegieter.
The number of people who can use schemes for poverty assistance should remain the same.
Young people should have their own meeting place in every village or town of the municipality of Beuningen.

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente Beuningen een eigen ontmoetingsplek hebben.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the police to more frequently monitor fatbike riders' behavior and speed.
There should be a bicycle tunnel to make sports park Ooigraaf more accessible.
statements.moreInfo: Sports park De Ooigraaf is located in the village of Beuningen.

statements.title: Visitors and organizers of events in the outlying areas should contribute to the conservation of nature in those areas.

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.
Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

titleParts.text: en moeten gaan meebetalen aan het behoud van de natuur in die gebieden.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should invest more in safer cycle paths at the lock in Weurt and at Pieckelaan.
A new residential neighborhood should be built between Beuningen-Oost and Weurt.
The center of Beuningen should become a pedestrian zone.
Each street in the municipality of Beuningen should have at least three trees.
The municipality of Beuningen should provide temporary shelter to asylum seekers who are not allowed to stay in the Netherlands.
The municipality should invest more in promotion to attract tourists to Beuningen.
Property tax (ozb) in Beuningen may not rise faster than the average inflation rate in the Netherlands.
The municipality should make preschool education free for all toddlers who need it.
Entrepreneurs must be able to apply for debt counseling while keeping their business.
The municipality of Beuningen should cooperate in allowing new wind turbines and solar parks.
The municipality of Beuningen should establish a citizens' assembly that advises the municipal council. A citizens' assembly consists of residents chosen by lot.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.
Each village in the municipality should receive its own budget to solve small problems in public spaces.
The municipality should provide subsidies to individuals for purchasing a home battery.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.
The municipality should encourage converting agricultural land in the municipality into nature areas.
The municipality should offer residents a course on how to use digital services.
The municipality should spend more money on temporary housing for young people, such as tiny houses or container homes.
The municipality should spend more money on sports and exercise for the elderly.
The municipality should make it easier to organize small-scale events in Beuningen.
The new construction of the De Lèghe Polder center should be combined with the building of apartments.
